Codepliant
PricingDocsGitHub

Free Tool

Free Cookie Policy Generator for Developers

GDPR and ePrivacy require you to disclose every cookie your application sets. Codepliant scans your code for cookies, local storage, session management, and third-party tracking scripts, then generates an accurate cookie policy.

What Codepliant detects

Most developers don't know every cookie their application sets. Third-party scripts, analytics SDKs, and authentication libraries all set cookies that need to be disclosed. Codepliant scans your source code and dependency tree to find them all.

document.cookie usage
Set-Cookie headers
express-session / cookie-parser
next-auth session cookies
localStorage & sessionStorage
Google Analytics cookies
Facebook Pixel
Advertising & retargeting pixels
Authentication tokens
CSRF tokens

Automatic cookie categorization

Strictly Necessary

Session cookies, CSRF tokens, authentication. These don't require consent under GDPR.

Performance

Analytics and monitoring cookies (Google Analytics, Mixpanel). Require consent.

Functional

Language preferences, theme settings, user customization. Require consent.

Targeting / Advertising

Ad tracking, retargeting pixels, social media cookies. Require explicit consent.

Generate your cookie policy

Free, open source, no account required.

npx codepliant go

Frequently asked questions

How does Codepliant detect cookies in my code?

Codepliant scans for cookie-setting patterns: document.cookie, Set-Cookie headers, express-session, cookie-parser, next-auth session cookies, and third-party scripts that set cookies (Google Analytics, Facebook Pixel, etc.). It also detects localStorage and sessionStorage usage.

Does the generated policy include cookie categories?

Yes. Codepliant categorizes detected cookies into: strictly necessary, performance, functional, and targeting/advertising. This categorization aligns with GDPR and ePrivacy Directive requirements for cookie consent.

Does Codepliant generate a cookie banner?

Yes. In addition to the cookie policy document, Codepliant can generate a cookie banner widget that you can embed in your application. The banner includes accept/reject options and links to your cookie policy.

Is this compliant with GDPR and ePrivacy?

The generated cookie policy includes GDPR-required information: what cookies are used, their purpose, duration, and whether they are first-party or third-party. It also includes consent language required by the ePrivacy Directive. We recommend legal review for your specific jurisdiction.
